req: REQ-028 (ai4good Claude Code Skill (the volunteer's single operating surface))

issues.detail: The updated requirement remains internally mixed-model: its conventions paragraph still says In Progress comes from self-assignment and a verified merge is the only Done path, its acceptance criteria say binding follows self-assignment, and its fallback says the Linear app covers every behavior. These contradict the d82 body, where PM pull binds and authorizes In Progress, PM complete authorizes Done, and merges affect dev items only.
issues.fix: Rewrite the helper, conventions, fallback, and acceptance-criteria language consistently: PM requirement pull -> binding/In Progress; verified complete -> PM Done; dev items -> vendor-native state including merge-to-Done; direct PM status edits are not authority.

issues.detail: The conflict-check PRD still asserts the single-tree model outside REQ-026/028, notably Story 5, REQ-008, REQ-012, REQ-036, and Technical Considerations: generic self-assignment/merge authorities, every P0 task completing by merge, one bootstrap task rather than the seeded PM tree, and status moving on PR merge. These conflict with the amended tests and make the declared PRD source of truth incoherent.
issues.fix: Fold d82 through all non-historical PRD sections: distinguish PM requirements from dev items, restrict NGO-facing state to PM, retarget project completion to completed PM requirements, and qualify merge automation as dev-tree-only.

issues.detail: “Pulls (assigns)” incorrectly reduces the new PM pull authority to assignment. A passing implementation could merely assign the requirement without recording the pull, moving it to In Progress through the authorized path, or establishing the worktree binding.
issues.fix: Assert that confirmation atomically records the PM pull, assigns the volunteer, authorizes In Progress, and establishes that requirement’s worktree binding; cancellation must do none of those.

issues.detail: Only the successful complete path is exercised. The test does not prove that missing linked dev merges, missing acceptance evidence, or evidence belonging to another PM requirement prevents PM completion.
issues.fix: Add negative fixtures for unmerged linked dev work, absent acceptance evidence, and merged/evidenced work linked to a different PM requirement; each must leave the pulled PM requirement In Progress and report the failed gate.

issues.detail: The clarifying helper remains anchored to an undifferentiated “task.” That permits a dev-tree item to become an NGO-facing anchor, leaking dev-tree working data across the d82 boundary.
issues.fix: Invoke it on a PM requirement. If invocation from a dev item is supported, require translation to its related PM requirement and prove that no dev-item title, status, or comment reaches the NGO-facing blocker/Q&A surface.

issues.detail: “Top-priority tasks done” retains the single-tree completion oracle and can pass by inspecting dev-tree status instead of authoritative PM requirements.
issues.fix: Retarget the readiness check to all top-priority PM requirements being Done through verified completion; dev-tree state may be reported only as supporting evidence, never as the project-completion authority.

issues.detail: The amended happy path proves dev merge-to-Done, but not the full dev-tree revert exemption or the hostile PM/dev identifier boundary. An implementation could exempt only merge events while wrongly reverting other vendor-native dev transitions, or let a branch/commit override carrying a PM identifier flip the PM item.
issues.fix: Add paired probes: vendor-native dev assignment/status/merge changes remain untouched, while the same GitHub or manual events aimed at a PM item never confer PM authority and are ignored or detect-and-reverted; the linked PM item remains governed only by pull/complete.

issues.detail: The manual matrix sends “pull a requirement” directly through the Linear PM team without proving an authorized pull record, despite d82 making arbitrary PM status edits revertible. It also routes all “in-progress tasks” to the public project page, which may expose dev-tree working items that must remain non-NGO-facing.
issues.fix: Name a manual PM-pull route that produces the same authoritative pull record without treating a raw Linear status edit as authority. Split context fallback explicitly: PM requirements/current work on the public page; dev-tree working items only in the volunteer’s Linear view.

issues.detail: The coverage map still says the Skill “drives merge” for completion and describes conventions as “branch->In Progress, merge->Done.” Both summarize the retired single-tree lifecycle and contradict AT-028.09/17.
issues.fix: Change the map to “complete verifies already-merged linked dev work plus acceptance evidence, then flips PM Done” and “dev branch/merge conventions affect dev items only; PM pull/complete are the sole PM authorities.”
